zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Le Shearman Sr.於1210年出生在英國肯特海時，他的父親Richard Le Sherman25歲，母親Christina Le Sherman21歲。他於1240年與Alice Layer在英國肯特海結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert Le Shearman Sr.于1210年出生在英国肯特海时，他的父亲Richard Le Sherman25岁，母亲Christina Le Sherman21岁。他于1240年与Alice Layer在英国肯特海结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Shearman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Sherman  . The surname is also established in Ireland (Kilkenny).

variants.name: Sherman
meanings: English (London): occupational name denoting someone who used shears to trim the surface of finished cloth and remove excessive nap, from Middle English sherman, shirman, sharman ‘shearman’.
Americanized form of North German Schürmann (see Schuermann ) and of German or Jewish (Ashkenazic) Schermann .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): occupational name for a tailor, from Yiddish sher ‘scissors’ + man ‘man’.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Le Shearman Sr. was born in 1210, in Hythe, Kent, England, his father, Richard Le Sherman, was 25 and his mother, Christina Le Sherman, was 21. He married Alice Layer in 1240, in Hythe, Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Le Shearman Sr. est né en 1210, à Hythe, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Richard Le Sherman, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Christina Le Sherman, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Alice Layer en 1240, à Hythe, Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert Le Shearman Sr. 1210 in Hythe, Kent, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Le Sherman 25 und seine Mutter Christina Le Sherman 21. Er hat 1240 in Hythe, Kent, England Alice Layer geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Le Shearman Sr. è nato il nel 1210 nel Hythe, Kent, England, il suo padre, Richard Le Sherman, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Christina Le Sherman, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Alice Layer nel 1240, nel Hythe, Kent, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert Le Shearman Sr.が1210年にイギリスケントhイスで生まれた時，父Richard Le Shermanは25歳，母Christina Le Shermanは21歳であった。1240年にイギリスケントhイスでAlice Layerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Le Shearman Sr.(이)가 1210년 영국 켄트 히테에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Le Sherman(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Christina Le Sherman(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1240년 영국 켄트 히테에서 Alice Layer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Le Shearman Sr. nasceu em 1210, em Hythe, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Le Sherman, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Christina Le Sherman, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice Layer em 1240, em Hythe, Kent, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Le Shearman Sr. родился в 1210 г. в местности хит, кэнт, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard Le Sherman было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Christina Le Sherman, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Layer в 1240 г. в местности хит, кэнт, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert Le Shearman Sr. nació en 1210, en Hythe, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Le Sherman, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Christina Le Sherman, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Alice Layer en 1240, en Hythe, Kent, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
